mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate above 1.5% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as rates rise in a tightening cycle.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.42% as of 2018-02-01 entering tightening phase
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS is rising month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate falls below 4.2% and continues declining, signaling tight labor markets and wage pressure that constrains corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between UNRATE decline and subsequent SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 3% year-over-year while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) decline more than 5% month-over-month, signaling demand rotation away from housing toward consumption that benefits discretionary equities, producing positive 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth > 3% AND HOUST declines > 5% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 492008M and HOUST at 1326K as of 2018-01-01
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or the joint signal (both conditions met) shows no significant predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) relative to either signal alone.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 1.5% year-over-year while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains above 2.5% year-over-year, signaling stagflationary pressure that reduces real returns and equity valuations, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth < 1.5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.24 and CPIAUCSL at 249.62 as of 2018-01-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (both conditions) adds no incremental predictive power beyond INDPRO alone (R² improvement < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 200K month-over-month for two consecutive months, signaling robust labor demand that supports consumer spending and equity valuations, producing positive 5-day returns as forward guidance improves.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth > 200K for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no significant outperformance versus baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1200K units, signaling demand weakness that reduces equity risk appetite, producing negative 20-day forward returns as growth expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 2.0% AND HOUST < 1200K
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17271.7B and HOUST at 1326K as of 2017-10-01 and 2018-01-01
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows no significant negative correlation with forward returns (correlation > -0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.8% year-over-year while federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 2.0%, signaling monetary policy lag and real rate compression that initially supports equities but triggers forward guidance concerns, producing negative 60-day returns as rate hike expectations build.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 249.62 and FEDFUNDS at 1.42% as of 2018-02-01
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the real rate gap signal (inflation minus FEDFUNDS) shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable below 2.5% year-over-year, signaling healthy supply-side growth without demand-pull inflation that supports equity valuations, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth > 2.5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.24 and CPIAUCSL at 249.62 as of 2018-01-01
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (both conditions) provides no incremental predictive power beyond INDPRO alone (R² improvement < 0.01).
